ogTitle: New Lambton Heights vs New Mexico: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in New Lambton Heights and New Mexico.
heading: New Lambton Heights vs New Mexico — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between New Lambton Heights, New South Wales and New Mexico, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: New Mexico has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to New Lambton Heights (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is New Lambton Heights or New Mexico safer from flooding?
faq.answer: New Lambton Heights has a flood risk score of 57/100, while New Mexico scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: New Lambton Heights scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. New Mexico scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in New Lambton Heights or New Mexico?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. New Mexico has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
